_note: Maps tool_name (or stem of .py file / plugin name) to a one-sentence human-readable description of what the tool looks for. Used by the Review UI to show a 'What this checks' hint when the scanner's raw description is empty or terse, and by the AI deep-triage prompt so the LLM knows what class of issue it's verifying. Keep each sentence under 25 words - space in the card is tight.
api_misuse_scanner: Detects C/C++ misuse of standard-library APIs: strncpy/strncat without NUL termination, snprintf returns used as offsets without truncation checks, and similar off-by-one patterns.
buffer_overflow_detector: Flags writes past fixed-size buffer bounds: memcpy/strcpy into stack arrays, loop counters beyond size_t guards, and alloca with tainted sizes.
auth_bypass_finder: Looks for authentication bypass patterns: missing permission checks on sensitive routes, hard-coded credentials, and broken isAdmin short-circuits.
boundary_check_scanner: Flags missing array/index bounds checks around user-controlled offsets before read/write.
callback_safety_scanner: Reviews function pointer and callback call-sites for missing null checks and signature type mismatches.
supply_chain_scanner: Looks for supply-chain smells: obfuscated strings, curl|bash install scripts, post-install hooks, and packages pinned to arbitrary git SHAs.
taint_flow_analyzer: Traces user-controlled inputs (argv, HTTP body, env) to sinks (exec, sql, unsafe deserialization) across function boundaries.
crypto_misuse_scanner: Detects weak or broken cryptography: MD5/SHA1 for auth, ECB mode, hard-coded keys/IVs, and rand() as a source of entropy.
race_condition_detector: Flags TOCTOU patterns, unprotected shared-state writes, and missing mutex/atomic operations in concurrent code.
format_string_scanner: Finds printf-family sinks that take user-controlled data as the format argument.
integer_overflow_scanner: Flags arithmetic that can wrap: multiplications into allocations, size_t/int mismatches, and signed/unsigned comparisons.
path_traversal_scanner: Flags file I/O that concatenates user input into paths without realpath / chroot / ../ stripping.
command_injection_scanner: Flags shell exec calls (system, popen, exec family, subprocess with shell=True) that include user-controlled strings.
sql_injection_scanner: Flags SQL composed by string concatenation of user input; suggests parameterized queries.
xss_scanner: Flags HTML output that reflects user input without contextual escaping (innerHTML sinks, direct echo in templates, unsafe React raw-HTML injection).
deserialization_scanner: Flags unsafe deserialization of user input (yaml.load, ObjectInputStream, unserialize, similar) that can trigger code execution.
ssrf_scanner: Flags server-side HTTP/TCP requests whose target is user-controlled without an allow-list or private-IP block.
cors_misconfig_scanner: Detects overly permissive CORS policies - wildcards with credentials, reflected origins, and null-origin acceptance.
xxe_scanner: Flags XML parsing without external-entity disabling (DOCTYPE, XXE, entity expansion DoS).
open_redirect_scanner: Flags HTTP redirects whose target is user-controlled and not validated against an allow-list.
file_upload_scanner: Flags file upload handlers missing MIME/extension validation, arbitrary path targets, and unsanitized filenames.
hardcoded_secret_scanner: Finds API keys, passwords, private keys, and JWT secrets committed to source.
weak_tls_scanner: Flags insecure TLS configurations: outdated protocols, insecure cipher suites, and disabled certificate verification.
input_validation_scanner: Finds endpoints/functions that operate on unvalidated user input.
cwe_pattern_matcher: Matches source against a curated library of historical CWE exploit patterns.
cve_pattern_hunter: Matches source against a database of published CVE patterns (buffer overflows, use-after-free, etc.) keyed by language + library.
config_audit_scanner: Audits config files (nginx, apache, ssh, docker, k8s) for known-insecure defaults and overrides.
iac_scanner: Reviews Terraform / CloudFormation / Bicep / Helm for insecure-by-default infrastructure patterns.
semgrep: Matches source against Semgrep's community + custom rulesets (OWASP top-10, language-specific CWE patterns).
trivy: Scans dependency manifests and container images for known-vulnerable packages and CVEs.
codeql: GitHub's semantic code-analysis engine - dataflow-based, language-aware queries for well-known vulnerability classes.
bandit: Python-specific static analyzer for common security issues (yaml.load, shell=True, weak random, etc.).
grype: Container-image vulnerability scanner - reports CVEs present in base image + layered dependencies.
osv-scanner: Cross-language dependency scanner backed by the OSV vulnerability database.
nuclei: Template-driven scanner for known CVEs + misconfigurations, typically against running services rather than source.
garak: LLM-specific vulnerability scanner - prompt injection, jailbreaks, data leakage from model outputs.
safety: Python-specific dependency scanner backed by pyup.io's vulnerability database.
